data.title: Gold rises for eighth time in nine sessions ahead of U.S.-Iran talks
data.description:
Gold futures gained Thursday with support from a weaker dollar, even as ongoing tensions threatened to derail a fragile ceasefire between the U.S. and Iran.

Israel continued to strike targets ​in Lebanon, and Iran maintained its blockade of the Strait of Hormuz, but precious metals rose anyway, perhaps because investors are growing more convinced that there may soon be a more permanent end to hostilities, Mizuho Securities strategist Robert Yawger said in a note.

"Price action implies traders are looking for this war to be over sooner rather than later," Yawger said.

A breakdown in negotiations between the U.S. and Iran and a renewed flare-up ‌in ⁠the war risk pushing up energy costs and inflation, which could force the Federal Reserve to keep rates higher for longer, thus lowering the appeal of non-yielding gold.

Gold should rebuild its gains and could retest record highs this year, Standard Chartered's metals research team said in a note, pointing to tentative signs that gold prices are stabilizing as ETF product flows turned positive in April.

Investors of such products tend to track real yields more closely than structural drivers, and if such flows stabilize, gold prices are more likely to find a floor, Standard Chartered said, adding that central banks also remain net buyers of gold.

Front-month Comex gold (XAUUSD:CUR) for April delivery closed up 0.9% to $4,792.20/oz, rising for the eighth time in nine sessions, and front-month Comex April silver (XAGUSD:CUR) ended up 1.4% to $76.277/bbl, the highest settlement value for both metals since March 18.

ETFs: (GLD), (GDX), (GDXJ), (IAU), (NUGT), (PHYS), (GLDM), (AAAU), (SGOL), (DUST), (RING), (BAR), (OUNZ), (SLV), (PSLV), (SIVR), (SIL), (SILJ)

data.title: Retractable Technologies cuts workforce to boost domestic production
data.description:
• Retractable Technologies (RVP) reduced its workforce by ~16% to improve efficiency and lower costs.
  • The move is expected to save about $2.2M annually, offset by ~$122K in one-time separation costs.
  • Around 58% of cuts impact manufacturing roles, with the rest in sales functions.
  • The company is increasing domestic production to reduce reliance on imports from China.
  • Tariffs on Chinese imports are driving the shift, though some dependence on China remains.

data.title: Cogeco GAAP EPS of C$1.89, revenue of C$693.56M
data.description:
• Cogeco press release (CGECF): Q2 GAAP EPS of C$1.89.
  • Revenue of C$693.56M (-7.9% Y/Y).

data.title: Empire Company acquires Mayrand Food Group to boost Québec retail push
data.description:
• Empire Company (EMP.A:CA) and its subsidiary Sobeys on Thursday said it has signed an agreement to acquire Québec-based food retailer Mayrand Food Group.
  • Financial terms of the deal were not disclosed.
  • The deal was positioned as an entry into Québec’s discount/warehouse food segment, serving both retail and foodservice demand, the company said.
  • The transaction is expected to close in Q1 FY2027.
  • The Mayrand brand will remain separate while benefiting from Empire's supply chain and operational support.

data.title: Genesis announces private placement of $120,000
data.description:
• Genesis AI (AIGFF) on Thursday announced a non-brokered private placement at a price of $0.06 per unit for net proceeds of $120,000.
  • Under the offering, the company will issue up to 2 million units. Each unit consists of one common share and one common share purchase warrant.
  • Each warrant entitles the holder thereof to acquire one common share at the price of $0.10 per share for a period of 24 months from closing.
  • The company intends to use the net proceeds from the offering to support general and administrative expenses and to pursue new business opportunities.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Starbucks Workers Union accuses company of negotiating in bad faith -- report
data.description:
The union representing Starbucks employees has filed a complaint with the U.S. Labor Relations Board accusing the coffee chain of negotiating in bad faith, making “punitive” proposals that the union couldn’t accept, and backtracking on seven items it had initially agreed to.

According to the complaint cited by Bloomberg, Starbucks Workers Union accused the company of acting “with the intent of preventing any agreement from being reached.

“Starbucks has returned to the bargaining table, but its behavior hasn’t improved,” the union said in a statement to Bloomberg.

In response, Starbucks claims it is “acting in good faith,” but that changes to any earlier agreements are “appropriate to reflect current business realities, customer expectations, and partner interests.

The union has been trying to secure a new contract with the company for more than four years, seeking a minimum hourly rate of $17 per hour, 4% annual raises, “just cause” protections, a fair dress code that allows union t-shirts, back pay to current and former baristas, and an end to “union busting.”

Starbucks, however, maintains that it offers industry-leading pay and benefits worth over $30 an hour, and  current demand for an increase in the minimum wage of hourly employees by as much as 77% over a three-year period is “unsustainable.”

Starbucks Workers Union represents ~600 of the company's 10K company-run stores.

Seeking Alpha has reached out to Starbucks for comment.

data.title: Tajiri Resources upsizes non-brokered private placement to C$2.5M
data.description:
• Tajiri Resources (TAJ:CA) on Thursday increased the size of its non-brokered private placement from C$1.5 million to aggregate gross proceeds of up to C$2.5 million through the sale of up to 13.88M units at a price of $0.18 per unit.
  • Each unit will consist of one common share in the capital of the company and one-half of one common share purchase warrant. Each whole warrant will entitle the holder to purchase one additional common share at a price of C$0.40 for a period of eighteen months from the closing date of the offering.
  • Closing of the offering is expected to occur on or about April 22, 2026.
  • The net proceeds from the offering are expected to be used for the exploration and development of the company's mineral projects, future acquisitions, and for general working capital purposes.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Xanadu Quantum Technologies Limited GAAP EPS of -C$14.29, revenue of C$4.62M
data.description:
• Xanadu Quantum Technologies Limited press release (XNDU): Q4 GAAP EPS of -C$14.29.
  • Revenue of C$4.62M.
  • Cash and cash equivalents of C$16.2 million as of December 31, 2025. Cash on hand combined with expected cash inflows was more than enough to see the Company through to the closing of the business combination.

data.title: Lockheed Martin secures $4.76B U.S. Army contract
data.description:
• Lockheed Martin (LMT) was awarded a $4.76B firm-fixed-price contract for the production of PAC-3 Missile Segment Enhancement missiles, including all incidental services, hardware, equipment, technical planning, management, and manufacturing efforts.
  • Work will be performed in Huntsville Alabama; Clearwater Florida; East Aurora New York; Rocket Center West Virginia; Vergennes Vermont; Hollister California; Wichita Kansas; Lake Mary Florida; Pinellas Park Florida; Camden Arkansas; Chelmsford Massachusetts; Grand Prairie Texas; Lufkin Texas; Ocala Florida; and Archbald Pennsylvania, with an estimated completion date of June 30, 2030.
  • Fiscal 2026 missile procurement, Army funds in the amount of $264.96M and fiscal 2026 Foreign Military Sales funds in the amount of $4.5B, were obligated at the time of the award.
  • The contracting activity is Army Contracting Command, Redstone Arsenal, Alabama.

data.title: Stocks to watch after market on Thursday: CRWV, CDNL, and KMX
data.description:
Wall Street’s major indexes finished higher on Thursday, as optimism around a potential resolution to the U.S.-Iran conflict continued to support investor sentiment. 

The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) gained 0.6%, while the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) rose 0.8% and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJI) added 0.6%.

Here are the three stocks to watch on the day:

CoreWeave (CRWV) shares rose 3.5% in after-hours trading after the company announced the pricing of a $1.75B private offering of 9.750% senior notes due 2031—an increase of $500M from the originally proposed size. The notes are set to mature on October 1, 2031, with the transaction expected to close on April 14, 2026.

Cardinal Infrastructure Group (CDNL) moved lower in after-hours trading by 1% after announcing it had secured a $24M contract to perform civil infrastructure work on a large-scale data center project. The award reinforces the company’s growing presence in high-demand infrastructure segments, particularly those tied to data center expansion. Construction is scheduled to commence in the second quarter of 2026, with project completion anticipated in 2027.

Shares of CarMax (KMX) are in focus after the company added two new members to its board of directors, including one nominee backed by an activist investor and a major shareholder. At Thursday’s annual shareholder meeting, the company appointed auto industry veteran Jim Kessler along with William Cobb, who was nominated by Starboard Value.
